component.name: PaymentCard
component.business_context: Merchant dashboard widget displaying a saved payment method, allowing the merchant to select a default card or delete a saved card.

states.name: default
states.a11y: Card is rendered as role="radio" within a role="radiogroup" container, aria-checked="false". Masked number exposed via aria-label="Card ending in {last4}" (visible text stays '**** **** **** {last4}'). Brand icon exposes an accessible name (e.g. aria-label="Visa") rather than aria-hidden. Delete control is a distinct, independently focusable Button with aria-label="Delete card ending in {last4}". Space/Enter toggles selection when the card is focused; visible focus ring via color.focus.ring on both the card and the delete button.

states.name: selected
states.a11y: Selection is never conveyed by color alone: a 2px border in color.action.primary.default is paired with a visible checkmark Icon (accessible name "Selected") next to the brand icon. role="radio" aria-checked="true" reflects the bound isSelected prop. Selection change is announced via a visually-hidden role="status" region (e.g. "{brand} ending in {last4} selected").

states.name: loading
states.a11y: Renders Skeleton placeholders (radius.sm) for brand icon, masked number, expiry, and cardholder name in place of real content; the card region gets role="status" aria-label="Loading payment card" so assistive tech announces the pending state without reading skeleton internals (aria-hidden on skeleton children). Card is not focusable/selectable and delete Button is disabled while loading.

states.name: empty
states.a11y: Renders a neutral message ("No saved payment method") plus a primary Button labelled "Add card" bound to onAddCard; no error styling/tone is applied. Region uses role="group" with an aria-label describing the empty payment-method slot; the Add-card Button is reachable via Tab and carries a visible focus ring via color.focus.ring.

states.name: error
states.a11y: Surfaces an Alert with tone="error" role="alert" containing errorMessage, announced immediately to assistive tech. If last-known card data (last4/brand/expiry/name) is present it is preserved and still rendered beneath the Alert rather than discarded. Card is not selectable while in error; delete Button remains available so the merchant can retry removal.

delete_flow.description: Deletion is never triggered by a single click. onDeleteRequest opens a confirmation surface (isDeleteConfirmOpen) implemented as an Alert/dialog with role="alertdialog" and an accessible name referencing the card (e.g. "Delete card ending in {last4}?"). Confirm calls onDeleteConfirm; Cancel or Escape calls onDeleteCancel and returns focus to the delete Button. While a delete request is in flight, isDeleting renders a Spinner (role="status", aria-label="Deleting card") in place of the confirm Button's label and disables both confirm and cancel controls. Success or failure is announced via a role="status" (success) or role="alert" (failure) region.

responsive.behavior: Below breakpoint.sm, the card stacks the brand icon above the masked number/cardholder name/expiry block (column layout, gap: space.2) instead of a leading-inline icon, to avoid truncating the masked number.

Below breakpoint.md, the action controls (select affordance and delete Button) reflow below the card details in a full-width row (gap: space.2) instead of sitting inline at the trailing edge of the card.
Below breakpoint.lg, the parent dashboard grid collapses from a multi-column card grid to a single-column stacked card list (each PaymentCard full width, gap: space.4 between cards).
Above breakpoint.xl, the card's max-width is capped and the card is centered within its grid cell instead of stretching to fill the full cell width.

accessibility: Card group is a role="radiogroup" of role="radio" PaymentCard items; aria-checked reflects the isSelected prop; Space/Enter selects the focused card.
Selection state is conveyed by a 2px color.action.primary.default border plus a visible checkmark icon (never by color alone), addressing WCAG 1.4.1.
Default/non-selected card boundary uses color.border.strong (not color.border.default) to meet the WCAG 1.4.11 3:1 non-text contrast minimum.
Body text (masked number, expiry, cardholder name) uses color.text.primary, not color.text.disabled, to meet the WCAG 1.4.3 4.5:1 text contrast minimum in the default (non-disabled) state.
Masked card number has aria-label="Card ending in {last4}" so screen readers announce a meaningful phrase instead of reading mask glyphs literally.
Card brand icon exposes an accessible name (e.g. aria-label="Visa") instead of the Icon component's aria-hidden default, since it conveys meaning.
Delete control is an independently focusable Button (not nested inside the card's own interactive/selectable role) with aria-label="Delete card ending in {last4}".
Deletion requires a confirmation step: role="alertdialog" surface with Confirm/Cancel; Escape cancels and returns focus to the delete Button.
Select and delete outcomes are announced via a visually-hidden role="status" (success/neutral) or role="alert" (failure) live region.
Visible focus-visible ring via color.focus.ring is applied to the selectable card, the delete Button, and the confirm/cancel controls.
Keyboard model: Tab moves between card body and delete control as separate stops; Enter/Space selects the focused card; Escape dismisses the delete-confirmation surface.
Any selection-highlight transition or delete/removal animation is wrapped in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) to disable or shorten it.
Delete-in-flight state shows a Spinner with role="status" aria-label="Deleting card" and disables confirm/cancel controls until the request resolves.
Loading state exposes role="status" aria-label="Loading payment card" on the card region while Skeleton placeholders are aria-hidden.
Error state uses Alert role="alert" and preserves last-known card data instead of discarding it.
open_questions: No token exists specifically for a 'selection' indicator; using color.action.primary.default (~4.0:1 on white, passes the 3:1 non-text minimum) as the selected border color, paired with a non-color checkmark cue, per gaps.a11y.json recommendation.

No token exists for the default (non-selected) card border that meets 3:1 contrast; color.border.default (#D8DEE9, ~1.3:1) fails WCAG 1.4.11, so color.border.strong (#A7B0C0) is used as the nearest existing token, though its exact contrast ratio against white was not independently re-derived here and should be confirmed by the token/QA validators.

No dedicated 'destructive' action token exists for the delete Button; using color.feedback.error for the delete affordance's icon/text tone as the nearest existing semantic match, falling back to standard Button variants (secondary/ghost) for structure.
No explicit token/spec value was given for the delete-confirmation dialog's overlay; color.surface.overlay is used as the nearest existing token.

components.json's Card primitive does not expose a native 'selectable/radio' variant; the radiogroup/radio semantics (role, aria-checked, keyboard handling) must be layered on top of Card by the code generator rather than assumed to exist in the primitive.
